class.name: ARCamera
class.brief_description: Provides camera tracking state and pose information from an AR session.
class.description: Wraps the AR camera data, including the device pose, tracking state, and on iOS, camera intrinsics and projection matrices. [b]Platform availability:[/b] - [b]iOS:[/b] Full support including intrinsics, projection matrices, exposure, and image resolution. - [b]visionOS/macOS:[/b] Only [member transform], [member euler_angles], [member tracking_state], [member tracking_state_reason], and [member timestamp] are available. Camera intrinsics, projection matrices, exposure, and image resolution return empty/zero values because the system compositor owns the camera pipeline and Apple does not expose lens parameters to applications.

method.name: project_point
method.description: Projects a 3D point to 2D screen coordinates. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns zero on visionOS/macOS because camera intrinsics are not available.

method.name: projection_matrix_for_orientation
method.description: Returns a projection matrix for the given orientation and viewport. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns empty on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: unproject_point
method.description: Unprojects a 2D point onto a plane in 3D space. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns zero on visionOS/macOS.

method.name: view_matrix_for_orientation
method.description: Returns a view transform for the given interface orientation. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns the default transform on visionOS/macOS.

members.member: The camera orientation expressed as Euler angles in radians.
Exposure time in seconds. [b]iOS 13+ only.[/b] Returns zero on visionOS/macOS.
Exposure EV offset. [b]iOS 13+ only.[/b] Returns zero on visionOS/macOS.
The camera image resolution in pixels. [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns zero on visionOS/macOS.
The camera intrinsic matrix (3x3). [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns empty on visionOS/macOS.
The camera projection matrix (4x4). [b]iOS only.[/b] Returns empty on visionOS/macOS.
The frame timestamp associated with this camera sample.
The current quality of camera tracking. See [enum TrackingState].
If tracking is limited, explains why. See [enum TrackingStateReason].
The camera transform in world space.

constants.constant: Camera tracking is available but currently limited.
No specific reason is reported for limited tracking.
Tracking is limited because the session is still initializing.
Tracking is limited because the device is moving too quickly.
Tracking is limited because the scene lacks enough visual features.
Tracking is limited because ARKit is attempting to relocalize in a known map.
